title: The EVA03 incident was very unconvincing and forced
display_title: The EVA03 incident was very unconvincing and forced
wikitext:
[]: The EVA03 incident was very unconvincing and forced             Quote Post by Slowpokeking » Wed Aug 21, 2024 10:26 pm I already got that feeling but now when I rewatch it, I want to openly speak about it. Everyone knows EVA01 was important, especially Gendo needs Shiji to finish his plan. WHY did they pick Toji, who is a close friend to him? Ok you got 11th angel who could control and infect. Unit04 was just destroyed. Shouldn't ppl be careful about Unit03? Why did they deliver Unit03 with such little care, not covering its body, directly went through a thundercloud(shouldn't flight avoid it usually?). Also they let both Misato and Ritsuko lost conscious and NO battleplan was made about REMOVE THE PLUG. This episode was crucial to the whole series' direction but the plot really felt a bit forced.

[]: Re: The EVA03 incident was very unconvincing and forced             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Thu Aug 22, 2024 6:41 am Slowpokeking wrote: Also they let both Misato and Ritsuko lost conscious and NO battleplan was made about REMOVE THE PLUG.

[]: Re: The EVA03 incident was very unconvincing and forced             Quote Post by Slowpokeking » Thu Aug 22, 2024 8:54 am Axx°N N. wrote: The very first thing that occurs to introduce the Angel is a sticky substance preventing the plug from ejection.

[]: Re: The EVA03 incident was very unconvincing and forced             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Thu Aug 22, 2024 9:42 am Slowpokeking wrote: And they could make a plan to take the plug out by force.

[]: Re: The EVA03 incident was very unconvincing and forced             Quote Post by Slowpokeking » Thu Aug 22, 2024 9:48 am Axx°N N. wrote: Slowpokeking#943615 wrote: And they could make a plan to take the plug out by force.

[]: Re: The EVA03 incident was very unconvincing and forced             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Thu Aug 22, 2024 10:48 am Ritsuko explains why they picked Touji; his core is ready to be prepared. Perhaps the other students' mothers' souls aren't ready yet.

[]: Re: The EVA03 incident was very unconvincing and forced             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Thu Aug 22, 2024 10:07 pm The whole point was that Toji was in Shinji's class precisely because he was a candidate. Maybe they wouldn't even be friends if he wasn't (as in, if he was in another class/school/city). Toji's father works for NERV and his mom is gone. There's way more to him than it seems... On the shipment of EVA 03, It was sent to Japan with less thought behind it (as opposed to EVA 02, which was heavily guarded) because the USA wanted to get rid of it fast. They literally just threw it in the hands of NERV Japan and that was it.

[]: Re: The EVA03 incident was very unconvincing and forced             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Fri Aug 23, 2024 12:09 am BernardoCairo wrote: On the shipment of EVA 03, It was sent to Japan with less thought behind it (as opposed to EVA 02, which was heavily guarded) because the USA wanted to get rid of it fast. They literally just threw it in the hands of NERV Japan and that was it.

[]: Re: The EVA03 incident was very unconvincing and forced             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Thu Aug 29, 2024 8:48 am I guess the Americans were desperate to get rid of EVA 03 And that makes sense. From their point of view, they were holding a super-divine atomic bomb that could make cities vanish. Besides, the series did everything to show that they are very pragmatic. In that conversation Gendo had with the Chinese guy on the plane, it's clear that they didn't want to help with the Evangelions initially. Then we learn from Misato that they agreed to help only on the condition that they would have exclusive production rights to EVA 03 and EVA 04. To me, it seems like they just didn't want to be left behind. They wanted a piece of the Evangelion's power and when that backfired, they took a step back. Perhaps there was also some economic interest in producing it domestically. But it didn't went well either way. They lost money and people. Axx°N N. wrote: It's funny to think that the takeaway from an Angel attacking from the depths made them reconsider their transport means, and so maybe they were like, "screw marine transport," only for something even worse to happen via the sky.

[]: Re: The EVA03 incident was very unconvincing and forced             Quote Post by MillionsKnives » Mon Sep 09, 2024 10:04 am Some people have raised the question of why Asuka and Rei are the only ones to revive training and not Shinji or Toji etc. This is because Nerv was hoping to have the dummy system fully operational before the angels awoke. So there was only the need to train Asuka as a stop-gap until then, with Rei as a backup. It just happened to be the angels were a little earlier than expected which meant the need to bring in others. And really considering everything happens in less than a year they were very close to their goal, with the dummy tested a couple of months later and being fully operational by EoE.

[]: Re: The EVA03 incident was very unconvincing and forced             Quote Post by BernardoCairo » Tue Sep 10, 2024 8:46 am MillionsKnives wrote: This is because Nerv was hoping to have the dummy system fully operational before the angels awoke. So there was only the need to train Asuka as a stop-gap until then, with Rei as a backup.

[]: Re: The EVA03 incident was very unconvincing and forced             Quote Post by Axx°N N. » Tue Sep 10, 2024 5:32 pm BernardoCairo wrote: It's a pretty cool theory, but I'm inclined to think they were planning on using the Dummy System in conjunction with the pilots at some point. They valued them highly and spent a lot of money on their recruitment and training. Plus, they just kept using them.

[]: Re: The EVA03 incident was very unconvincing and forced             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Nov 01, 2024 6:32 pm Axx°N N. wrote: It's funny to think that the takeaway from an Angel attacking from the depths made them reconsider their transport means, and so maybe they were like, "screw marine transport," only for something even worse to happen via the sky.

[]: Re: The EVA03 incident was very unconvincing and forced             Quote Post by brandneweyes » Fri Dec 20, 2024 3:43 pm In-regard to transportation. A little silly; I know the show likes the crucifix, but... lol, uhh, maybe wanna tuck the arms in? Lol. Right? WE'RE FLYING! THIS ISN'T A PARADE. Of course fandom can say it's a matter of size, but still...
